title: campanula decumbens subsp. decumbens
intro: Campanula decumbens subsp. decumbens, also known as creeping bellflower, is a type of flowering plant in the bellflower family that grows low to the ground with bell-shaped flowers. The plant is believed to have medicinal properties, with its leaves and flowers said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Campanula decumbens subsp. decumbens include its potential use in treating wounds, reducing fever, and easing respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
